channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Iran’s sports minister rules out national team taking part in 2026 World Cup over war and security concerns
item.description: Iran’s Minister of Sports and Youth, Ahmad Donyamali, has declared that the country’s national football team will not participate in the 2026 FIFA World Cup, which is scheduled to be held across the U...

item.title: Mark Carney’s International Engagement Must Be Matched by Accountability at Home
item.description: Since assuming office, Prime Minister Mark Carney has undertaken an ambitious international agenda, travelling extensively to forge strategic partnerships and enhance Canada's global presence. Within ...

item.title: Karachi observes Youm-i-Ali with heavy security arrangements
item.description: KARACHI: On Wednesday, Karachi witnessed the main Youm-i-Ali procession, marking the martyrdom of Hazrat Ali, with very strict security measures. The procession began at Nishtar Park and concluded at ...

item.title: Tinubu Sets Up Committee to Oversee New Grid Asset Management Company
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu has set up an 11-member committee to help incorporate the proposed Grid Asset Management Company Limited (GAMCO), wey go help solve issues wey dey affect electricity grid m...

item.title: UNSC Adopts Resolution Calling for Iran to Stop Attacks on Gulf States
item.description: On 12th March 2026, the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) adopted a resolution led by Bahrain condemning Iran’s missile and drone attacks on Gulf countries, including US assets. The attacks were ...

item.title: US troops suffer severe injuries including brain trauma from Iranian drone strike on Kuwait port facility
item.description: An Iranian drone strike targeted a US logistical support unit at the civilian Shuaiba port in Kuwait on 1 March 2026, killing six American Army Reserve troops and injuring dozens more during the early...

item.title: Army Neutralises Senior Lashkar-e-Taiba Operative During LoC Infiltration Attempt
item.description: In Jammu and Kashmir’s Rajouri district, the Army successfully neutralised a Pakistan-backed terrorist during an infiltration attempt along the Line of Control. The deceased has been identified as Ham...

item.title: Farooq Abdullah survives assassination attempt; J&K CM Omar Abdullah questions security measures
item.description: On Wednesday night, Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister Omar Abdullah reacted to a serious incident involving his father, Farooq Abdullah, the National Conference president. During a wedding event where ...

item.title: Catholic Priest Criticizes Daniel Bwala for Focusing on Peter Obi During Al Jazeera Interview
item.description: Rev Fr. Kelvin Ugwu, a Catholic missionary priest wey dey The Gambia, don yan him mind about the kain wahala wey follow Daniel Bwala interview for Al Jazeera. Fr. Ugwu talk say instead of dey address ...

item.title: Additional Legal Challenges Filed Against NTSA's Automated Traffic Fines
item.description: Three more petitions have been lodged in the Kenyan courts challenging the National Transport and Safety Authority's (NTSA) new instant fines system for traffic violations. This automated platform, wh...

item.title: Suspended Crime Intel Head Testifies on Contact with Matlala at Madlanga Commission
item.description: Maj-Gen. Richard Shibiri, the suspended head of organised crime intelligence, appeared before the Madlanga Commission on Wednesday to answer questions about his interactions with alleged tycoon Vusimu...

item.title: Louis Theroux Explores the Influence and Profitability of the Manosphere Online
item.description: Louis Theroux, acclaimed documentarian known for exploring extreme and controversial subcultures, turns his focus to the online 'manosphere' in his new Netflix documentary, Inside the Manosphere. The ...

item.title: Islamophobic incident at Ballarat Iftar prompts police and political response
item.description: A disturbing Islamophobic incident occurred at a Ramadan Iftar dinner in Alfredton, Ballarat, Victoria, where a 37-year-old man allegedly forced his way into the community hall, threatened children ou...

item.title: Prime Minister Nchemba to inspect key development projects during Katavi Region visit
item.description: Prime Minister Mwigulu Nchemba is set to begin a four-day official visit to Katavi Region, starting on March 13 and ending on March 16, 2026. According to Katavi Regional Commissioner Mwanamvua Mrindo...

item.title: NDP MP Alexandre Boulerice Considers Move to Québec Solidaire Amid Party Challenges
item.description: After Nunavut MP Lori Idlout crossed the floor to join the Liberals, attention has shifted to Quebec MP Alexandre Boulerice and his political future. Boulerice has publicly stated that he is seriously...

item.title: China expands trade and infrastructure cooperation with Africa amid strained US relations
item.description: China has unveiled a major plan to strengthen its partnership with Africa, offering full zero-tariff access for all African imports starting 1 May. This initiative forms part of the 'China-Africa Year...

item.title: PNP assures police services remain uninterrupted despite energy-saving measures
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) has assured the public that its police operations and frontline services will continue uninterrupted despite implementing energy-saving initiatives. This move come...

item.title: 67-year-old grandmother maintains youthful appearance through regulated exercise and microdosed weight-loss medication
item.description: Bobbi Parker-Hall, a 67-year-old grandmother from Oregon, has shared how she maintains a youthful appearance and a toned figure, crediting a combination of regular strength training, proper nutrition,...

item.title: High Court confirms dismissal of terrorism charges against Kneecap rapper
item.description: The High Court has upheld the dismissal of a terrorism case against Liam Og Ó hAnnaidh, known professionally as Mo Chara, a member of the Belfast-based rap group Kneecap. The charges arose from an all...

item.title: 20-year-old charged following alleged Sydney tobacconist kidnapping
item.description: Police in Sydney have charged a 20-year-old man in connection with the alleged kidnapping of another man outside a local tobacconist. The incident has drawn public attention due to its sudden and alar...
